Theatre Trips Booking Procedure for Outings and Theatre Trips PLEASE DO NOT PAY FOR TRIPS BEFORE YOUR BOOKING HAS BEEN CONFIRMED • Bookings can be made at Open Meetings or through the St Ives Cambs. u3a website: • Theatre: Kathy Meadows https://stivescambs.u3asite.uk/u3a_groups/theatre-trips/ • Outings: Sue Belcher https://stivescambs.u3asite.uk/u3a_groups/outings/ • (07778425052 —Strictly for members with no internet) • When booking a trip on the web page, click on the blue envelope, please DO NOT click on 'CONTACT' at the very top of the page. • ALWAYS BOOK BEFORE PAYING. All bookings will be confirmed. • BACS payment preferred please, either online or by going into your branch • Payment: St Ives (Cambs) U3A Social Account • Sort code: 40-16-08 Account: 74557352 • Ref: u3a membership number and trip name • If no alternative, cheques made payable to: St Ives (Cambs) U3A Social Account • Payment to be made at least six weeks prior to the trip Nutcracker in Havana Friday 17th January 2025 Evening Performance Milton Keynes Theatre A Cuban take on the favourite festive classic, Carlos Acosta’s brand new production Nutcracker in Havana explodes onto the stage, Tickets £48.50 plus travel WAITING LIST ONLY https://stivescambs.u3asite.uk/ u3a_groups/theatre-trips/ 2 Kinky Boots, the Musical Wednesday 12th March 2.30 pm Milton Keynes Theatre Please note that Johannes Radebe will not be appearing in this matinee performance After inheriting his family's failing shoe fac- tory and with a relationship on the rocks, life is proving very challenging for Charlie Price. That is until he meets Lola, a drag queen whose sparkle and unsteady heels might just hold the answer to saving the struggling business. Ticket money £41 to be paid by 20 th January, travel costs to be advised

Outings 3 Anglesey Abbey Thursday 28th January Visit the house and gardens A Jacobean-style house full of rich history, from a monastic priory to 20th-century home. A garden for every season including a winter garden, herbaceous border, rose garden, formal garden and dahlia garden. £14 for NT Members, £30 others National Stud & Bury St Edmunds Tuesday 4th March Morning visit to the National Stud at Newmarket with coffee on arrival followed by a guided coach tour of the National Stud stopping from time to time to admire the horses and foals. Also hear about the history of the stud, and an overview of the work that goes into producing the future champions of the turf. Travel to Bury St Edmunds for lunch (not included) Free Time to explore the Abbey Ruins and Abbey grounds or shop or both! National Aboretum (near Lichfield ) Tuesday 15th April Arrive in time for coffee (not included). Morning Land Train Tour See the splendour of the Arboretum from the comfort of their covered land train with an accompanying 50- minute pre-recorded commentary. Lunch  (not included ) picnic or in the café. Afternoon free time to explore the grounds. More detail and cost for both new visits will be advertised at the January Open Meeting and on our webpage

Christmas round up 4 Many groups enjoy a get-together at Christmas. Here are just a few of the events that took place in December. Petanque Over 60 members and partners sat down for our lunch at a very festive St Ives Golf Club. We all took part in a Christmas quiz and enjoyed food from a varied menu. We were entertained by Carol, Grace and Maggie singing a song written by Carol to the tune of ‘All Things Bright and Beautiful’. There was a lucky draw where the winners won French wine or cheese. The Golf Club looked after us superbly and we look forward to returning next year. Ron Meadows Swedish Gill from the Swedish Group prepared a ‘julbodet’ (Christmas feast) for the group at her home. They enjoyed plenty of Swedish food and games. Pia Consoli Art Appreciation The Art Appreciation group enjoyed a not-so-festive lunch at The Tap Room, but quite delicious all the same and lots of fun. Anne Bannell Table Tennis We had our Christmas lunch at Slepe Hall. Twenty-one mem- bers attended and an enjoyable afternoon was had with lovely food, great company and a few quizzes to keep everyone entertained. Christine Blackmore Philosophy Group The philosophy group held its Christmas party in the Axe and Compass where everyone had a jolly time figuring out some puzzles and a Christmas themed quiz. The group reflected on a successful year and that planning was in hand to produce some original seminars and the play they are putting on in the Corn Exchange on 6th September. A good time was had by all. Roy Moyser
